headline: Ford Exits EVs As Toyota Move In
summary: Ford (NYSE: F) left the EV business for dead earlier this year. It does appear it will enter again, on a small scale, next year. At the same time, Toyota (NYSE: TM), which sells about as many cars in the US as Ford does, is pressing to expand its EV fleet. Someone is making a ... Ford Exits EVs As Toyota Move In

headline: What Caused Ford's 14% Y/Y U.S. Sales Decline in April?
summary: F's April U.S. sales slide 14% as weak EV, hybrid, truck and SUV demand, plus macro pressures, offset gains in select models like Bronco.

raw.headline: What Caused Ford's 14% Y/Y U.S. Sales Decline in April?
raw.summary: F's April U.S. sales slide 14% as weak EV, hybrid, truck and SUV demand, plus macro pressures, offset gains in select models like Bronco.

headline: LCID Q1 Earnings Miss Estimates on Inventory Write-Downs
summary: LCID posts wider-than-expected Q1 loss and misses estimates as write-downs, costs and supplier issues hit margins, even as revenues grow and liquidity gets a boost.

headline: Tesla Has Recalled 219,000 Cars This Year. How That Compares to Ford and Toyota.
summary: Tesla  stock rose early Wednesday as the electric-vehicle maker recalled 218,868 cars to correct a rearview camera issue.  Tesla shares were up 0.2% at $390.26, while  and  futures were up 0.7% and 0.9%, respectively.  Benchmark  oil prices  were down 8% at just over $101 per barrel in early trading.

headline: TXU Energy EV charging program could work in other competitive markets: ChargeScape CEO
summary: Ford electric vehicle drivers get 15 hours per day of “free” home charging under a special retail plan in Texas. The automaker says it shifted 515 MWh of energy to off-peak periods in 2025.

headline: Ford CEO Jim Farley Says Industry Must Make EVs 'More Affordable' As New-Car Costs Strain Buyers
summary: Ford Motor Co. CEO Jim Farley says making vehicles more affordable must become a priority for the auto industry, arguing that Ford is already preparing lower-priced models as new-car costs strain U.S. households. Farley Says Ford Must Lower Prices In a CBS News interview on Friday, Farley was asked whether Americans can afford new cars today. "Some Americans can," he said. "But we need to do a great job as a brand and as an industry to make our vehicles more affordable. I think you’re certainly
